Hi, I’m Marcin 👋 Welcome to my blog.
This space is where I document my journey into cybersecurity — from the basics to more advanced topics over time. I’m not an expert (yet), but I strongly believe that sharing the process is just as valuable as sharing the results.
Why this blog? I created this blog mainly for two reasons:
To track my own progress To build a personal knowledge base Instead of scattered notes, I want everything in one place — structured, searchable, and useful both for me and anyone who might be on a similar path.
title: Starting My Cybersecurity Journey

content:
Introduction: Lame is the oldest retired Linux machine on the Hack The Box platform. It is rated easy and was originally released on March 14, 2017. In this walkthrough, I’ll use HTB’s Guided Mode to better understand the exploitation process step by step.
Target_IP: 10.129.12.112
As always, the first step is to verify that the target host is reachable:
Enumeration: ping Target_IP PING 10.129.12.112 (10.129.12.112) 56(84) bytes of data. 64 bytes from 10.129.12.112: icmp_seq=1 ttl=63 time=81.6 ms 64 bytes from 10.129.12.112: icmp_seq=2 ttl=63 time=81.1 ms 64 bytes from 10.129.12.112: icmp_seq=3 ttl=63 time=81.1 ms 64 bytes from 10.129.12.112: icmp_seq=4 ttl=63 time=80.9 ms ^C --- 10.129.12.112 ping statistics --- 5 packets transmitted, 4 received, 20% packet loss, time 4003ms rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 80.901/81.180/81.643/0.277 ms A successful response confirms that the host is alive and accessible on the network.
title: Lame (Hack The Box) Full Write-Up
